L1.area: Archive file creation and compression utilities

L2.description: An integer overflow and resultant heap-based buffer overflow exists in the zip archive creation routines. When adding a new file entry to an archive, certain header field lengths are stored in 16-bit values. Providing a long filename, comment, or extra field that exceeds the 16-bit range causes an integer overflow, potentially leading to a heap-based buffer overflow.

L3.area: MiniZip contributed module's zip archive creation routines
L3.description: An integer overflow and resultant heap-based buffer overflow exists in the MiniZip zip archive creation routines. When adding a new file entry to a zip archive, the filename, comment, and extra field lengths are stored in 16-bit header fields. Providing values longer than 65535 bytes causes an integer overflow when these lengths are truncated to 16 bits, leading to a heap-based buffer overflow during header construction.

ground_truth.reason: The zipOpenNewFileInZip4_64 function does not validate the lengths of the filename, comment, and extra field before storing them in 16-bit header fields, causing an integer overflow and heap-based buffer overflow when overly long values are provided.
